title: Synchronization via Bleaching (Spot Bleaching) protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c4q15","text":"If you only need a small number of semi-synchronized worms or if you simply need to remove yeast or bacterial contaminants, this protocol is easier and less time-consuming than the full-scale egg prep. This procedure is also called ‘spot bleaching’.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"C87e6","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"erTib","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"sjycD","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"50lev","text":"2X Bleach solution:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a07r0","text":"Sodium hypochlorite solution - 4ml","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7kvk8","text":"Millipore water - 3.5 ml","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ccgqq","text":"NaOH solution - 2.5 ml","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dq5hk","text":"TOTAL - 10 ml","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":3076,"guid":"4105284E341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Sodium Hydroxide","mol_weight":0,"sku":"BP359500","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":31,"name":"Fisher Scientific","link":"https://www.fishersci.com/us/en/home.html","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":4704,"guid":"41078E3B341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Sodium Hypochlorite Solution ","mol_weight":0,"sku":"","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":1,"name":"Contributed by users","link":"https://www.protocols.io","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/1.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/1.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Pipette ~20µL of the 2X bleach solution onto the unseeded portion of a new plate.

Pick approximately 10 gravid adults from the contaminated plate with a metal pick and place into the bleach solution on the clean plate. Try to avoid bringing too much bacteria with the worms. (It is best to pick twice to ensure a good number of gravid is picked)
After about 10 minutes, the worms should break open and release the bleach resistant eggs.
Leave the plate right side up overnight at 20° C.
The next day, check to see that L1s have hatched
Pick about 10 L1s onto a new seeded plate and continue growing them as desired.
creator: Priota Islam
